name: VALHI INC /DE/
business: Valhi, Inc. is a holding company operating through subsidiaries in three segments: Chemicals (Kronos Worldwide, Inc., a producer of titanium dioxide pigments), Component Products (CompX International Inc., a manufacturer of security products and marine components), and Real Estate Management and Development (Basic Management, Inc. and The LandWell Company, which develop land in Henderson, Nevada).
sellsTo: Commercial, industrial and residential land buyers
namedSuppliers: Rio Tinto Iron and Titanium Ltd

exposures.sentence: Kronos is a leading global producer and marketer of value-added titanium dioxide pigments.
Kronos owns and operates an ilmenite mine in Norway pursuant to a governmental concession with an unlimited term.
LandWell is engaged in efforts to develop certain land holdings for commercial, industrial and residential purposes in Henderson, Nevada.
The first homes in our Cadence planned community were completed by third-party builders and sold to the public.

CompX is a leading manufacturer of security products used in the postal, recreational transportation, office and institutional furniture, cabinetry, tool storage, healthcare applications and a variety of other industries.

CompX is also a leading manufacturer of wake enhancement systems, stainless steel exhaust systems, gauges, throttle controls, trim tabs and related hardware and accessories for the recreational marine and other industries.
Technology failures or cybersecurity breaches could have a material adverse effect on our operations.

catalysts.event: Approval of remaining infrastructure reimbursement notes
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to receive approval for the remaining infrastructure reimbursement notes receivable up to the $170 million cap in 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of large community-wide infrastructure projects
catalysts.sentence: Construction on several of these large projects began in late 2021 and is expected to be completed in 2027.

catalysts.event: Recognition of all remaining deferred revenue
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to recognize all remaining deferred revenue during 2026.

tamClaims.claim: TiO2 consumption has grown at a CAGR of approximately 2-3% since 2000
tamClaims.sentence: According to industry estimates, TiO2 consumption has grown at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 2-3% since 2000.

tamClaims.claim: Demand for TiO2 is expected to grow by 2-3% per year long-term
tamClaims.sentence: Over the long-term, we expect demand for TiO2 will grow by 2% to 3% per year, consistent with our expectations for the long-term growth in GDP.
